hypothesis: Unified profiling reveals tool bottlenecks invisible to per-tool timing. Expect orient.py to be dominant cost.
method: Built tools/swarm_profiler.py: times 18 operations (5 git, 8 tool subprocesses, 5 filesystem). Thresholds from L-637/L-688 baselines. Wall-clock via time.monotonic(). WSL2 environment.

results.surprise: task_order.py (14.7s) exceeds orient.py (11.0s) — newest tool is slowest. validate_beliefs.py (10.9s) also twice threshold. orient.py NOT dominant — it's third.

verdict: CONFIRMED — profiling revealed task_order.py as #1 bottleneck (not orient.py). 79% of 56s overhead is tool subprocess execution. Filesystem I/O is negligible (0.03s). git operations are moderate (5s, WSL penalty). Tool persists results for trend tracking across sessions.
